design_philosophy.core_loop: Navigate maze, place bombs to destroy blocks and defeat enemies, collect power-ups, progress through themed stages
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Time pressure and spatial awareness—managing bomb placement while avoiding explosions in confined spaces with multiple threats
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Strategic puzzle-action hero clearing danger zones by timing explosions and manipulating the environment
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Deliberately scaled back gameplay complexity from predecessors (Bomberman '94, Super Bomberman 2) to return to 'classic formula'—rejecting feature creep in favor of accessibility and focused core loop. Expanded multiplayer identity (5-player support, country-themed characters) positions it as party game alongside single-player story

difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Multiplayer modes allow up to 5 players, suggesting scaling difficulty through player count and character selection

progression.upgrade_system: Power-up collection during stage progression (bombs, speed, detonation radius implied by series conventions)
progression.unlock_mechanism: Story mode progresses through element-themed stages; Five Dastardly Bombers act as boss encounters unlocking subsequent worlds
replayability_hooks: 5-player Battle Mode with country-themed character roster
Multiple playable characters (White Bomber, Black Bomber, Pretty Bomber, country representatives)
Story mode replay for narrative completion
Competitive multiplayer with varied character abilities

world_building_techniques: Element-themed star worlds (fire, water, ice, etc.) serving as visual differentiation
Resurrection narrative: Five Dastardly Bombers revived by Bagular
Character representation through country assignments (Pretty Bomber = France)
Boss hierarchy: Five Dastardly Bombers → Bagular as final antagonist

boss_design.philosophy: Five Dastardly Bombers serve as recurring antagonists tied to narrative throughline; each likely occupies stage-end position within themed worlds. Bagular functions as overarching final boss representing villain resurrection plot
notable_bosses: The Five Dastardly Bombers (resurrected antagonists from Super Bomberman 2)

faction_indicators: Country-based character roster (each fighter represents Earth nation)
White Bomber vs Black Bomber dynamic suggesting hero/rival positioning
Pretty Bomber as France representative—first non-enemy role signals character diplomacy
danger_signals: Bagular's UFO incursion represents external threat requiring unified response
Five Dastardly Bombers' resurrection indicates past conflicts resurfacing
environmental_storytelling: Element-themed worlds reflect controlled domains under Five Bombers' influence
Junkyard setting (Bagular's UFO location) implies forgotten/discarded threat
Visual simplification compared to predecessors grounds story in accessible, symbolic presentation
